01 — Client Project

The Latest Scoop

Brand Narrative Realignment

Context

Canadian lifestyle and home retailer in leadership transition, seeking to realign brand narrative for an evolving audience.

Challenge

Refresh the brand story, retain legacy language, and support a widening audience without alienating the existing customer base.

Constraints

Retention of legacy language. Existing agency work required selective integration due to tone misalignment. Client required intentional humor where appropriate.

Deliverables

  • Revised Brand Story — multiple directions
  • Updated Brand Documentation

Original

"A lifestyle concept store offering a curated selection of pretty things for you and your home: fashion, home decor, accessories, furniture, stationery, giftware and much more."Legacy copy
"Our Purpose is to inspire our people and our customers to embrace their individuality through genuine interactions."Legacy copy

Revised

"All the pretty things of Paris, Rome, and London. All our favorite designs from New York to Madrid, from Los Angeles to Bali. All the beauty and meaning for you and your home.

Beginning in 2004 as a series of pop-ups, The Latest Scoop has grown to eight permanent locations based in our favorite Vancouver, Victoria and Toronto neighborhoods.

As a lifestyle concept store, our passion is to bring you the freshest looks, from our travels, week to week. Carefully curated, our stores are as seasonal and inspiring as they are beautiful."Sam Broadway

02 — Spec Project

Inner Form Studio

Narrative + Positioning

Overview

A narrative studio reconnecting organizations to purpose through language. When it comes to language, we believe function follows form.

Voice

Compassionate Measured Grounded Accessible

Works with

Organizations in cultural transition. Founders seeking greater meaning. Brands with a public conscience.

Does not work with

Companies who pursue profit at the expense of people.

Deliverables

  • Brand Narrative / Positioning Document
  • Narrative Style Guide
  • Key Web Copy
  • Messaging Framework
  • Stewardship Outline & Agreement

03 — Spec Project

Single Form Studio

Object Design + Product Copy

Studio Overview

An object design studio that employs repetition and constraint to cultivate purpose.

Core Idea

We repeat the simple forms of a sphere and a cylinder. By adhering to this constraint, we deepen our attention to emerging function.

Voice

Restrained Considered Elegant

Flagship Product

An incense burner that catches ash and stores it long term, reducing mess and frequency of cleaning. Ash falls onto the curved surface of a sphere, guided into the hidden well below, and collects over time.

Key Lines

Because the best part is beginning again.
From silence a form, from stillness a function.
Designed with your attention in mind.

04 — Spec Project

The Well on Main

Brand · Copy · Design System · Digital

Overview

A comprehensive spec project for a contemplative yoga and meditation studio in Richmond, VA's Fan District. Positioned against the shallowness of mainstream wellness — grounded in textual lineage, pedagogy, and the serious transmission of Eastern contemplative practice.

Voice

Warm, assuring Composed, staid Pedagogical Curious, relevant Considered, precise

Deliverables

  • Brand Narrative + Positioning
  • Mission Statement
  • Weekly Schedule
  • Event Description
  • Homepage Copy
  • About Page Copy
  • Newsletter — The Sip, Issue 01
  • Welcome Email
  • Figma — Desktop + Mobile Homepage
  • Figma — About Page, Desktop + Mobile
  • Figma — Style Guide
  • Social Mockups — Two Instagram Posts
  • Newsletter Mockup — Substack
  • Welcome Email Mockup

Case Study

The Well on Main began as a positioning problem: how do you build a brand for a yoga and meditation studio that takes itself seriously — that insists on lineage, on transmission, on the difference between practice and exercise — without sounding precious or exclusionary?

The answer was to lead with conviction rather than aspiration. Where mainstream wellness brands speak in the language of self-improvement — optimize, transform, become — The Well speaks in the language of inheritance. The tools already exist. The lineage is real. The work is to pass it on.

This informed every decision downstream: the choice of Cormorant Garamond, a typeface with genuine historical weight; the muted palette anchored in warm neutrals; the copy that never promises transformation but always implies depth.

The homepage opens not with a CTA but with a statement: True transformation only begins within. The word "begins" carries the whole philosophy — nothing is completed here, only initiated.

The newsletter, The Sip, was conceived as an extension of the Thursday Night Talks — not a marketing vehicle but a continuation of the studio's intellectual project. Issue 01 takes the smartphone as its subject and arrives, via the Yoga Sutras, at compassion rather than critique.

The design system was built to hold all of this: generous whitespace, squared corners, a parallax structure that lets images breathe. Every element in service of the feeling that something serious is happening here.

Newsletter — Full Text

The Sip — Issue 01
Notes from Zen & the Art of Scrolling

Is it possible that there is more to this automatic habit? When we reach for our phones, is it only the object, the world it contains, we are reaching for?

We have come to know this as addiction, one we love guiltily and hate bitterly. It is a tool we use for a job we didn't decide upon. Sometimes we need, even deserve, a little doom, an excuse to do next to nothing. Constantly inundated is the new being bored. But the scroll doesn't stop once we've lost time for it. It grabs hold of us when we want most to be free of it.

We think because it's so new there is nothing we can do about it. But the smart phone is just the latest container for a forever problem, one with an equally infinite solution.

The Yoga Sutras describe this very problem as one foundational to the practice itself. Chitta vritti — the wandering mind, the restless, grasping mind. This is not a description of a flaw, but a description of its very nature. Our mind does not exist without this quality, this ability to become distracted. And not simply to become distracted, but to generate more and more distractedness.

At the time the Sutras were written, the container for the mind's distraction was quite different. As populations grew, cities proliferated, marketplaces spawned — new forms on which to place one's evermore fractured attention, more commerce, more things, more noise. The mind sought refuge in its own external reflection.

Knowing this, it is somewhat less surprising that even novel reading was, in the 19th Century, considered by some to be nothing more than an inebriating, debilitating addiction. Of course, we think we know better now.

Today, we look at our phones and we see a cause. If it weren't for this, I would be focused, I wouldn't lose time, I wouldn't get off track. But this device is just taking advantage of our own inherent restlessness. We look for it — at times desperately — and it finds us. But we were already wandering long before it arrived.

This new thing is not the source of our problem but its mirror. No tool has ever before so effectively captured the ceaseless movements of the mind. With this understanding, we can begin to view this device with compassion; rather than the thief of our desire, it is its most accurate map.

Each time we reach for it, what are we really reaching for? Connection? Stimulation? Validation? Joy? Escape? If we reach a thousand times a day, is this not a thousand moments to examine why?

There is immense gratitude to be discovered in such small moments, if we can make them less automatic. By honoring the pull as proportional to the need it conceals, by asking ourselves what this need really is, we expand this moment and offer an opportunity for return.

Welcome Email

Mission

We are here to deepen the lives of the individuals living in our community, to ground their awareness in a practice, passed on. From one, shared space, we move the world.

Key Lines

True transformation only begins within.
Our schedule doesn't change, so yours never has to.
Begin at The Well.
